questions.q: What kind of being is Sugriva in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sugriva is a vanara, one of the monkey-folk who allied with Rama.

questions.q: Which kingdom did Sugriva rule?
questions.explanation: Sugriva became king of the vanara realm of Kishkindha.

questions.q: Who was Sugriva's elder brother and rival?
questions.explanation: Sugriva's elder brother Vali drove him into exile and was his rival for the throne.

questions.q: Which hero did Sugriva form an alliance with?
questions.explanation: Sugriva allied with Rama, who helped him regain his kingdom.

questions.q: What did Sugriva contribute to Rama's cause?
options: The vanara host to search for Sita
questions.explanation: Sugriva lent the vast vanara army to search for the abducted Sita.

questions.q: Why was Sugriva living in exile when Rama met him?
options: He had been cursed by a sage
His brother Vali had driven him out
He had renounced the throne willingly
questions.explanation: Vali had expelled Sugriva from Kishkindha, forcing him into exile.

questions.q: Which devoted vanara was Sugriva's loyal minister and companion?
questions.explanation: Hanuman was Sugriva's minister and the one who first introduced him to Rama.

questions.q: On which mountain did Sugriva take refuge from Vali?
questions.explanation: Sugriva sheltered on Mount Rishyamukha, which Vali could not enter due to a curse.

questions.q: What was Sugriva searching for on Rama's behalf?
questions.explanation: Sugriva mobilized the vanaras to find Sita, who had been carried off by Ravana.

questions.q: Who killed Vali so that Sugriva could reclaim his throne?
questions.explanation: Rama slew Vali with an arrow, restoring Sugriva to the throne of Kishkindha.

questions.q: Who was the divine father of Sugriva according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Sugriva was said to be born of Surya, the sun-god, while Vali was born of Indra.

questions.q: What sign did Sugriva send Hanuman to investigate when Rama and Lakshmana approached?
options: A flying chariot in the sky
questions.explanation: Fearing they were Vali's agents, Sugriva sent Hanuman to learn who the two strangers were.

questions.q: What proof did Sugriva show Rama that Sita had passed that way?
options: A bundle of jewels and garments she dropped
questions.explanation: Sugriva produced ornaments Sita had cast down from Ravana's chariot as she was carried over Kishkindha.

questions.q: How did Rama first demonstrate his strength to reassure Sugriva before their pact?
options: By piercing seven sal trees with a single arrow
questions.explanation: Rama shot one arrow through seven sal trees, convincing Sugriva he could defeat Vali.

questions.q: Why did Rama strike Vali from concealment rather than in open combat?
options: Vali drained half the strength of any foe he faced
questions.explanation: Vali had a boon that took half an opponent's strength, so Rama struck from hiding to ensure his fall.

questions.q: In the first duel, why did Rama fail to shoot Vali?
options: Vali fled before he could aim
He could not tell Vali and Sugriva apart
questions.explanation: The brothers looked alike, so Rama could not distinguish Sugriva from Vali in the melee.

questions.q: What was tied around Sugriva's neck in the second duel so Rama could identify him?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana placed a garland of Gajapushpi (flowering creeper) on Sugriva to mark him out.

questions.q: After regaining his throne, what did Sugriva initially do that angered Rama and Lakshmana?
options: He gave away the kingdom to Angada
He delayed the search for Sita amid pleasures
questions.explanation: Sugriva indulged in pleasures and forgot his promise, provoking Lakshmana's wrath until he resumed the search.

questions.q: Who was Sugriva's chief wife and queen after he became king?
questions.explanation: Ruma was Sugriva's wife, whom Vali had earlier seized during the brothers' feud.

questions.q: Whom did Sugriva appoint as crown prince (yuvaraja) of Kishkindha after Vali's death?
questions.explanation: Sugriva made Angada, Vali's son, the heir apparent of Kishkindha.

questions.q: In which direction did Sugriva send the party led by Hanuman and Angada to seek Sita?
questions.explanation: Sugriva dispatched the southern search party, which ultimately learned Sita was in Lanka.

questions.q: What deadline did Sugriva set for the vanaras to return from the search for Sita?
questions.explanation: Sugriva ordered the search parties to return within one month, on pain of death for failure.

questions.q: What was the original cause of the deadly rift between Sugriva and Vali?
options: Vali's pursuit of the demon Mayavi into a cave
A quarrel over the throne's succession
An insult before the vanara court
questions.explanation: Vali chased the demon Mayavi into a cave, and the misunderstanding over his fate split the brothers.

questions.q: What did Sugriva do at the cave's mouth that Vali never forgave?
options: He sealed it with a boulder, believing Vali dead
He set fire to the entrance
questions.explanation: Seeing blood and hearing no reply, Sugriva blocked the cave with a stone and returned, which Vali took as betrayal.

questions.q: Why could Vali not enter Mount Rishyamukha to attack Sugriva?
options: A boon of Brahma protected the hill
A curse of the sage Matanga barred him
The mountain was made of fire
questions.explanation: The sage Matanga had cursed Vali to die if he set foot on Rishyamukha, making it Sugriva's refuge.

questions.q: Which dying figure had earlier directed Rama and Lakshmana toward Sugriva?
questions.explanation: The slain demon Kabandha, freed from his curse, advised Rama to seek Sugriva's friendship.

questions.q: Whose throwing of the demon Dundubhi's carcass had Vali once boasted of, prompting Rama to test his own strength against it?
questions.explanation: Vali had hurled the bones of the slain buffalo-demon Dundubhi, and Rama kicked the skeleton far to prove his might to Sugriva.

questions.q: Who pleaded with Sugriva to make peace and avoid war with Vali, fearing the loss of her husband?
questions.explanation: Tara, Vali's wise queen, counseled against the conflict, foreseeing the doom it would bring.

questions.q: When Lakshmana came in anger to the palace over the delayed search, who calmed him and pacified the quarrel?
questions.explanation: Tara went out to meet the enraged Lakshmana and soothed him, allowing Sugriva to resume his duty.

questions.q: During the final war, what notable feat did Sugriva himself perform against the rakshasas?
options: He fought and wounded Ravana in single combat, tearing his crown
He built the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: Sugriva leapt upon Ravana, grappled with him, and tore away his crown before being thrown off.
